svg.title: Personalized ML + GenAI Agent roadmap
svg.desc: A 6-phase roadmap from ML foundations to production AI agents, tailored for someone with Databricks ETL and AWS data engineering experience.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What Databricks Delta Lake and ETL concepts should I solidify before moving into ML?')
g.text: You are here — Databricks ETL + Delta Lake + AWS migration
Notebooks, pipelines, Delta tables, basic Spark — already in your hands

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Give me a 2-week plan for ML foundations, focusing on what a Databricks ETL engineer needs most — skip what I likely already know from data engineering')
g.text: Phase 1 — ML foundations (2–3 weeks)
Skip heavy math — you need applied intuition, not proofs
· Scikit-learn: regression, classification, clustering, cross-validation
· Spark MLlib on Databricks — train models on your existing Delta tables
· MLflow tracking (experiment logging, model registry) — built into Databricks

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Give me a 3-week deep learning plan using PyTorch on Databricks. What projects should I build as an ex-AWS data engineer?')
g.text: Phase 2 — Deep learning with PyTorch (3 weeks)
Build intuition first — Karpathy videos are the best starting point
· Neural nets, backprop, CNNs, training loops in PyTorch
· Run training jobs on Databricks GPU clusters — use what you have at work
· Project: train a model on your own migrated data (anomaly detection, classification)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Give me a 3-week plan to learn transformers and LLMs, focused on practical use on Databricks and Azure OpenAI')
g.text: Phase 3 — Transformers + LLMs (3 weeks)
This is where your pipeline skills become directly valuable
· Attention, BERT, GPT — HuggingFace course (free, excellent)
· Azure OpenAI API: completions, embeddings, function calling
· Fine-tuning a small LLM on Databricks using your Delta tables as training data

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Give me a detailed plan to build a RAG pipeline on Databricks with Azure OpenAI. What vector databases work best?')
g.text: Phase 4 — RAG pipelines (2 weeks) · fastest job-ready win
Your ETL + Delta experience makes this feel natural
· Chunking, embedding, vector stores (Databricks Vector Search or pgvector)
· Build: RAG chatbot over your company's internal Delta Lake data
· LangChain + Azure OpenAI + Databricks — the full production stack

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I build AI agents on Databricks using LangChain and Azure OpenAI? Give me a 3-week project-based plan')
g.text: Phase 5 — AI agents (3 weeks)
Tool-calling, memory, multi-step reasoning over your data
· LangChain / LlamaIndex: ReAct, function calling, agent memory
· Databricks AI agent framework + Azure OpenAI as the brain
· Project: data pipeline agent that monitors, debugs, and fixes Delta jobs

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does MLOps look like on Databricks and Azure ML? Give me a practical checklist to productionize my ML models')
g.text: Phase 6 — MLOps + production (2 weeks)
You already know CI/CD and infra — this will feel familiar
· MLflow model registry, Databricks Model Serving, A/B testing
· Azure ML pipelines, drift detection, retraining triggers
· Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) for deploying ML jobs like code

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the best order to take these certifications: Databricks ML Associate, Azure DP-203, Azure AI-102?')
g.text: Total: ~16–18 weeks · 1 hr/day on weekdays + weekend projects
Certs: Databricks ML Associate (after Phase 3) · Azure AI-102 (after Phase 5)
You can skip DP-203 — your AWS + Databricks work already covers it
